clues: While first officially recognized in the 1970s, evidence of the bacteria causing this disease has been found in prehistoric remains

In the mid-1970s, mothers living in Connecticut noticed an unusual outbreak of arthritis in children. Their advocacy led to the discovery and subsequent naming of the disease after the town where this occurred

The classic rash, although not present in all cases, forms as bacteria spread outward. As the immune system attacks the bacteria at the center, surviving bacteria migrate outward, leaving a red ring and central clearing
In the 2nd century, the Greek physician Galen coined the name of this disease meaning 'flow of seed'
Before modern medicine, physicians tried injecting toxic metals directly into the urethra to treat this disease

The diplococci causing this disease has been developing resistance to nearly every antibiotic class used against it since the 1940s. If untreated, this infection can lead to infertility, ectopic pregnancies, or epididymitis

In the 1940s, German scientists were working on creating food substitutes as part of the war effort and created a mixture of triglycerides that tasted a bit like butter. Analyzing this, they isolated this drug and accidentally discovered its additional medical properties

This medication binds to free carnitine and is excreted, leading to depletion of the body's carnitine stores, reducing ATP required for the urea cycle and build up of ammonia. Because of this, you can treat the hyperammonemia with levocarnitine supplmentation
Commonly used to treat seizures, it can also be used to treat bipolar disorder, and prevent migraines
Because this medication has a distinct unpleasant natural odor, many manufacturers intentionally coat the tablets with a mint scent to mask the smell
A World Health Organization (WHO) Essential Medication for cardiac indications, this medication can also be used in PCOS and hormonal acne

This medication binds to receptors in the collecting ducts of the kidneys, preventing production of epithelial sodium channels and sodium-potassium ATPase pumps. It also acts as an anti-androgen leading to gynecomastia
Common in older cats who often show signs of increased thirst and weight loss, this is a leading cause of human death worldwide
As early as 100AD, Roman physicians used hot baths to sweat out toxins as a treatment for this disease which they diagnosed by distinct symptoms
In late stages, a unique white crystalline residue can appear on the skin, often around the face
